paragraphs.body: . A. Writing Exploration: Write a longer summary of your chosen resource Start Assignment In the article “The History of the Hermes Birking Bag and how it became so expensive” published without an author name on the “Glad Observer,” the author reports the history of one of the most expensive bags supplied to the most elite group of consumers. They believe that to invest in one of their bags is better than to invest in gold. Hermes bags, especially Birkin, are “unmatched by any luxury fashion house.” This reputation was build based on how exclusive and expensive they are. Hermes reject the idea to release the number of Birkin bags that will be manufactured each year because they want to “tight control over the distribution of the bags along with their intricate craftsmanship keeps the bag’s desire alive.” The article acknowledges the idea of luxury, using as example the appearance on the big screen, in the TV show named “Sex and the City” which, in 90s, targeted the young wealthy white women. Big names from the A-list as Victoria Beckham, Kris Jenner, Kate Moss, and Kim Kardashian own collections of over one hundred bags, estimated to over two million dollars.

annotations.context_left: and Kim Kardashian own collections of over one hundred bags, estimated to over two million dollars.

paragraphs.body: My childhood was a good one. My parents and my grandma took care of me. They razed me the best way that they could ever do. I am so grateful for it. My family was not a poor one but at the same time either a rich one. It was exactly enough to offer me everything that I was need it. My grandma was taking care of me most of the time when my parents was at work. She was next to me in one of the most important moments in my life: when I made the first steps, when I spoke for the first time, when I finished my elementary school, my high school. She is my best friend. The one that all the time I go when I have a tough time or if I need some advice. She thought me a lot of lessons, especially how to take care of my things. In grandma’s view, “eventing that is around you is expansive.” Most of the time, when I was little, I was questioning myself what she wanted to suggest with the expression. Now, at twenty years old, I get it more than ever.

annotations.comment: Lovely details and tone in this paragraph. Just  be sure you're using the right words :)
annotations.context_left: what she wanted to suggest with the expression. Now, at twenty years old, I get it more than ever.

paragraphs.body: The world expensive, if we search it one Google, on Oxford Languages website verify concept of “costing a lot of money.” This is what school taught me. This is how the society see the world “expensive.” But my grandma saw it with other eyes. The eyes of a nostalgic person. I commemorate the time when she was making me beanies, especially in winter. I loved them. I was warning them all the time, even in the house. It was fun the idea to have them in every colour, with different designs, and exactly how I want it. She was using woollen thread, not a pricey product, especially in Romania. For me this not just some accessories, it was the symbol of love, comfort and warmth. Especially, in today’s society, grandma’s beanies make me to acknowledge the idea that the true value of something isn’t just money. A lot of times I believe that most of the people forget that some of the most valuable possessions aren’t define by the price and by the memories.

annotations.text: For me this not just some accessories, it was the symbol of love, comfort and warmth.

paragraphs.body: The “take-off” fashion industry, especially today, is one of the most popular one. The new concept of “dupes” is really in trend right now. From my point of view, I have a love-hate relationship with it. As a student, I really love this idea. To be able to create cool, trendy outfits is a good opportunity. To express my passion for fashion, to find easier my style, to experiment more is an awesome opportunity. Simulations, this concept of “knock-off”
bothers me. I believe that they, kind of in a way cut the process of creativity. Is easier to copy someone idea then to create a new one.

annotations.text: believe that they, kind of in a way cut the process of creativity. Is easier to copy someone idea then to create a new one.
annotations.comment: this is an interesting idea, though I'm not sure how it's connected to the idea of "everything" being expensive and the value of things beyond money. Is this section necessary for the central focusing idea of your essay as a whole.

paragraphs.body: I believe that fashion is created by curiosity. On a personal level, being tall, a student-athlete, a volleyball player already for thirteen years old, it is a big challenge. That's because most of the time I need to wear sporty clothes. In this case is way harder to explore my style beyond what’s practical for sports. Being in this situation, most of my free time, even if I have the simplest task to do, I always choose to have a good outfit on me. Another challenge that I faced it was the idea of balance between self-expression and the option that I have. For sure, having such beautiful legs, the options are cut at the half, but even with that I notice that is way easier to find small businesses, created by tall women, then it was in the past. This is what I love about fashion, makes you to feel good without caring how you look like.

annotations.comment: I love the ideas in this paragraph, but how are they related to money and the value of clothing?

paragraphs.body: Is the idea of “expensive” a lie? It really defines us? I was thinking a lot of times about these questions. Why do we have this urge to show people how much money we spend on a bag, on what we have on us? I do not deny the fact that everything depends on us, as society how manipulate our way of thinking. Everyone has different opinions when it comes to this subject. But what is the real problem? The need of attention? The need to be seen by people that are around us? The need to be accepted in certain groups of people? To make the ones around us to want to be in our place? Or is the idea of “fake success “the one that make us happy? On another hand, we buy these all-expensive items for the ones around us or for us? In our generation, most of the people, try to find the attention with the help of how people see them from the exterior, not what is inside them. They do not care anymore of their behaviour, how they react to certain experiences. All it matters to them is how much their outfit cost, or when they cross a street everyone to look at them for a long time. In the end of the day, expensive and the value of the product is really who we are? https://www.asimplemodel.com/bips/bip-feed/the-cost-to-manufacture-a-basic-entry-level-hermes-birkin-handbag

annotations.text: The need of attention? The need to be seen by people that are around us? The need to be accepted in certain groups of people? To make the ones around us to want to be in our place? Or is the idea of “fake success “the one that make us happy?

annotations.comment: Nice revision! Great exploration of "fashion" from different perspectives and a lot of interesting ideas about the value of things. I love your pictures, too :) See my notes in-text for comments. Good work!
